Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze an event log of an Insurance Claims Processing process I need to identify cases with performance issues long durations analyze attributes Resource Region Complexity to find root causes and propose explanations and mitigation strategies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Analyze the Data I need to calculate the total duration for each Case ID and look at the flow of activities within each case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2001 Start 2024 04 01 09 00 Submit End 2024 04 01 10 30 Close Duration 1 5 hours Complexity Low Region A Flow Submit Evaluate Approve Pay Close No Request Additional Documents

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2002 Start 2024 04 01 09 05 Submit End 2024 04 02 11 00 Close Duration 26 hours Complexity Medium Region B Flow Submit Evaluate Request Additional Documents Approve Pay Close 1 Request loop Wait check the gap Evaluate 09 45 to Request Docs 14 00 is 4h Request Docs 14 00 to Approve 04 02 10 00 is 20h This is a significant wait time

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2003 Start 2024 04 01 09 10 Submit End 2024 04 03 09 30 Close Duration 48 hours Complexity High Region A Flow Submit Evaluate Request Additional Documents Request Additional Documents Approve Pay Close 2 Request loops Wait check the gaps Evaluate 09 40 to Req 1 11 00 1h 20m Req 1 11 00 to Req 2 17 00 6h Req 2 17 00 to Approve 04 02 16 00 23h Approve 04 02 16 00 to Pay 04 03 09 00 17h

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2004 Start 2024 04 01 09 20 Submit End 2024 04 01 10 45 Close Duration 1 hour 25 mins Complexity Low Region B Flow Submit Evaluate Approve Pay Close No Request Additional Documents

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2005 Start 2024 04 01 09 25 Submit End 2024 04 04 14 30 Close Duration 77 hours Complexity High Region B Flow Submit Evaluate Request Additional Documents Request Additional Documents Request Additional Documents Approve Pay Close 3 Request loops Wait check the gaps Evaluate 09 50 to Req 1 11 30 1h 40m Req 1 11 30 to Req 2 04 02 17 00 29 5h Req 2 04 02 17 00 to Req 3 04 03 15 00 22h Req 3 04 03 15 00 to Approve 04 04 10 00 19h

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Identify Performance Issues Case 2001 1 5 hours Fast Case 2002 26 hours Slow Case 2003 48 hours Very Slow Case 2004 1 25 hours Fast Case 2005 77 hours Extremely Slow Conclusion Cases 2002 2003 and 2005 are the performance issues Specifically 2005 is the worst followed by 2003 then 2002 2001 and 2004 are standard baseline

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Complexity Low 2001 2004 Fast less than 2 hours Medium 2002 Slow 26 hours High 2003 2005 Very Slow 48h and 77h Correlation Higher complexity correlates directly with longer duration and more Request Additional Documents loops

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Resource Adjuster Adjuster Mike Region A High Low Case 2001 Low Fast Case 2003 High Slow Adjuster Lisa Region B Medium High Case 2002 Medium Slow Case 2005 High Very Slow Observation Adjuster Lisa handled the two slowest cases 2002 and 2005 Adjuster Mike handled one fast and one slow

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis Lisa might be less efficient at gathering initial docs or has a higher workload leading to longer wait times between requests Or she is assigned the more difficult cases

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Region Region A 2001 2003 One fast one slow Region B 2002 2004 2005 One fast two slow Observation Region B has the longest case 2005 and a medium case 2002

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait lets look at the Request Additional Documents wait times Case 2003 Region A Req 1 to Req 2 6h Req 2 to Approve 23h Case 2005 Region B Req 1 to Req 2 29 5h Req 2 to Req 3 22h Req 3 to Approve 19h Comparison Region B Lisa seems to have longer wait times between the Request activities and the final Approve

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Process Flow The Request Additional Documents Loop Low Complexity 0 loops Medium Complexity 1 loop High Complexity 2 to 3 loops Key Insight The primary driver of delay is the number of Request Additional Documents activities This is driven by Complexity

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2005 High took 3 requests Case 2003 High took 2 requests Why the difference Adjuster Lisa Case 2005 requested docs 3 times Adjuster Mike Case 2003 requested docs 2 times This suggests Adjuster Lisa might be stricter less skilled at initial assessment or the Region B process for High Complexity claims is less efficient

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Performance Issues Cases 2002 2003 2005 Root Cause 1 Complexity High Medium complexity triggers the Request Additional Documents loop Low complexity bypasses it Root Cause 2 Resource Region Efficiency Adjuster Lisa Region B is handling the most complex cases 2002 2005 and generating the most requests 1 and 3 respectively Root Cause 3 Process Loop The process allows multiple iterations of Request Additional Documents without a cap or escalation path causing exponential delays

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Draft Recommendations Standardization Create checklists for Adjusters to ensure all docs are requested at once reduce loops Training Train Adjuster Lisa or Region B team on initial assessment to reduce the need for multiple requests SLA Thresholds Implement a maximum number of document requests before escalation to a Senior Adjuster Automation Use AI OCR to validate documents immediately upon submission Workload Balancing Ensure High Complexity claims are not all funneled to one resource Lisa

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review and Refine Check timestamps again Case 2005 Req 1 Day 1 11 30 to Req 2 Day 2 17 00 Thats a long gap Req 2 Day 2 17 00 to Req 3 Day 3 15 00 Another long gap Case 2003 Req 1 Day 1 11 00 to Req 2 Day 1 17 00 Same day Req 2 Day 1 17 00 to Approve Day 2 16 00

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Crucial Distinction Region A Mike was able to send a second request on the same day as the first Region B Lisa waited until the next day for the second request and the next day for the third This indicates a Resource Region specific efficiency issue not just complexity Correction Complexity causes the need for requests but the Resource Region determines the speed of handling those requests

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait one more check on Case 2002 Start 09 05 End 04 02 11 00 Gap Evaluate 09 45 to Req 14 00 Req 14 00 to Approve 04 02 10 00 The gap between Req and Approve is 20 hours Case 2001 Low Approve 10 00 to Pay 10 15 Case 2004 Low Approve 10 00 to Pay 10 30 The biggest delay is definitely the Request Additional Documents loop

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Plan Calculate durations Highlight the Request loop as the main delay factor Contrast Adjuster Mike Region A vs Adjuster Lisa Region B handling High Complexity Suggest training process caps and automation
